_meta.author: Claude (va-legislature-refine overnight autopilot)
_meta.note: VA House of Delegates batch, districts 83-87 (state tier). Otto Wachsmann (R, HD-83), Nadarius Clark (D, HD-84), Marcia Price (D, HD-85), Virgil Thornton Sr. (D, HD-86), Jeion Ward (D, HD-87). Evidence-based finalization from primary roll-call records (LIS/VPAP) + issue scorecards (ReproRising, Giffords, ChoiceTracker). KEY DEVIATION: Wachsmann voted YES on the 2025 marriage-equality amendment (HJ9, one of 7 GOP yes votes) then REVERSED to NO on all 2026 readings (HJ3 + companions) -- both documented. Clark, Price, Ward = uniformly progressive HARD voting records. Ward earns one genuine cross-pattern CREDIT (christian_liberty: YES on HB131 religious-accommodation in higher ed, 94-2). Thornton = freshman (elected Nov 2025, seated Jan 2026): scored from his own cited public statements + one HARD chief-patron bill (HB773 ballot-curing expansion); thin record, dispute form is the public backstop.

profile.religion: Self-identifies broadly as Christian on campaign materials; specific denomination not publicly stated
profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-83 (Sussex/Surry/Southampton/Dinwiddie area) since the 2023 redistricting; previously HD-75 (2022-2024). Registered pharmacist; owner of Stony Creek Pharmacy; former pharmacy-school faculty; volunteer fireman. Reelected Nov 4, 2025; term through Jan 2028.

otto-wachsmann.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-83 (R), sitting since 2022 (HD-75 then HD-83). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Genuinely conservative HARD voting record: consistent pro-life (voted NO on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment HJ1/SJ247 in BOTH 2025 and 2026 readings; ChoiceTracker rating 'Anti-Choice'; voted for born-alive protection). Strong 2A (NO on every Democratic gun-control bill -- assault-weapon/magazine bans, waiting periods, red-flag expansions, storage mandates, industry-liability). Election-integrity posture (NO on absentee/early-voting expansion, NO on automatic registration, NO on the felon-re-enfranchisement amendment HJ2). Anti-sanctuary on immigration (YES on HB77 authorizing local enforcement of federal immigration law; NO on HB1441/SB783 restricting local ICE cooperation). Tough-on-crime/pro-police (YES on Line-of-Duty-Act, retired-LEO protections; NO on marijuana sentence-leniency). KEY DEVIATION (verified roll calls): voted YES on the 2025 marriage-equality constitutional amendment (HJ9, 01/14/2025 -- one of only 7 GOP yes votes; and SJ249 companion) -- biblical_marriage q0 scored FALSE -- but REVERSED in 2026, voting NO on HJ3 2nd reference and all companion vehicles (SJ3, HB612, SB311) -- q1 scored TRUE with the reversal noted. Christian-liberty, school-choice/ESA, CBDC/sound-money, 10th-Amendment, E-Verify: no discrete recorded floor vote located -> left null, not inferred.

0.note: Consistent pro-life record: voted NO on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (HJ1/SJ247) in both 2025 and 2026; ChoiceTracker rating 'Anti-Choice'; voted YES on born-alive infant protection.

Voted YES on the 2025 marriage-equality constitutional amendment (HJ9, 01/14/2025; one of only 7 Republicans voting yes) and the SJ249 companion -- did not affirm marriage as one man and one woman that session.

1.note: REVERSED in 2026: voted NO on the marriage amendment HJ3 (2nd reference) and on all 2026 companion vehicles (SJ3, HB612, SB311), opposing codification of same-sex marriage. (Reversal from his 2025 YES -- both votes documented.)

2.note: Voted NO on expanding absentee/early voting and ballot-cure windows (HB773, HB774, SB438, SB58, SB582 in 2026; HB2056 in 2025) -- favors limiting absentee voting.

3.note: Voted NO on automatic/back-end voter registration (HB319, SB658) and on mail-in/early-voting expansion -- opposes mass mail-in expansion.

0.note: Affirmatively pro-law-enforcement: voted YES on Line of Duty Act benefits (HB1815/SB1142), retired-LEO protections (HB1874/SB781), LEO military leave; voted NO on civilian-oversight disclosure and LEO face-covering restrictions.

1.note: Voted NO on marijuana-offense sentence-modification/leniency bills (HB2555 2025; HB26/SB62 2026) -- opposes soft-on-crime leniency.

2.note: Anti-sanctuary: voted YES on HB77 (2026) authorizing state/local enforcement of federal immigration law (90-7); voted NO on HB1441/SB783 which would restrict local cooperation with federal immigration authorities.

1.note: Voted NO on every Democratic gun-control bill across both sessions -- assault-weapon/magazine bans (HB1607, HB217, HB1524), 5-day waiting periods, red-flag/'substantial risk' expansions, storage mandates, and firearm-industry-liability bills.

profile.religion: Not publicly stated (no denomination found on Ballotpedia, Wikipedia, VPAP, or campaign site)
profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-84 (Portsmouth/Suffolk area) since Jan 2024; previously HD-79 (2022-2023). Community organizer, radio host, former Biden 2020 regional operations director; BFA Virginia Union University. Chairs the Public Safety Firearms subcommittee. Reelected Nov 4, 2025; term through Jan 2028.

nadarius-clark.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-84 (D), sitting since Jan 2024 (formerly HD-79). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive HARD record, NO conservative deviation found. Voted YEA on the reproductive-freedom amendment (HJ1) in 2025 and 2026 -- A+/'Champion' from ReproRising; endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates. Voted YEA on the same-sex-marriage amendment (HJ9 2025, HJ3 2026). CHIEF PATRON of the 2026 assault-firearms-and-magazine ban (HB217, became law) and chairs the Firearms subcommittee; Giffords-characterized gun-safety champion. Chief patron of HB26 (marijuana sentence-modification/leniency, became law). Sponsor of the $15 minimum-wage step bill (HB1928). Election-integrity, Christian-liberty, immigration, federal-overreach cells: no individually-verified roll call located -> left null (his LIS member-votes tab would fill these in a future pass).

0.note: Voted YEA on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (HJ1) in 2025 and 2026; A+/'Champion' from ReproRising -- does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Voted the full pro-abortion-access slate (ReproRising A+) -- opposite of abortion-abolition legislation.

0.note: Voted YEA on the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (HJ9 2025, HJ3 2026) -- does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: Voted YEA to codify same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

2.note: Sponsor of HB1928 (2025) stepping the minimum wage to $15 -- expansionary wage-mandate / spending posture.

1.note: Chief patron of HB26 (2026), marijuana-offense sentence-modification (became law, CHAP1103) -- leniency direction, contrary to opposing soft-on-crime policy.

0.note: Per Giffords, voted for mandatory safety training as a condition of firearm purchase -- contrary to permitless/constitutional carry.

1.note: CHIEF PATRON of HB217 (2026), the assault-firearms-and-large-capacity-magazine ban that became law (CHAP1106); chairs the Firearms subcommittee.

profile.religion: Episcopalian — fourth-generation member of St. Augustine's Episcopal Church, Newport News; M.A. Religious Studies (Howard), M.Div. (Virginia Union)
profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-85 (Newport News/Hampton) since Jan 2024; previously HD-95 (2016-2024). Chair of the House Privileges & Elections Committee (first Black woman to chair it). Daughter of former Newport News Mayor McKinley Price; niece of U.S. Rep. Bobby Scott; openly pansexual. Reelected uncontested Nov 4, 2025.

marcia-price.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-85 (D), sitting since 2016 (HD-95 then HD-85); Chair of House Privileges & Elections. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Strongly progressive HARD record, NO conservative deviation. ReproRising A+/'Champion'; co-patron of the reproductive-freedom amendment (HJ1) and CHIEF PATRON of the Right to Contraception Act (HB1716). Lead Democratic voting-rights legislator: CHIEF PATRON of the Voting Rights Act of Virginia (HB1890, 2021), CHIEF PATRON of HB968 (2026, bars hand-counting machine-readable ballots), and a chief patron of the 2026 felon-re-enfranchisement amendment -- her P&E committee advanced all four 2026 constitutional amendments. Giffords-endorsed; chief sponsor of legislation enabling localities to enact their own gun measures. Marriage: openly pansexual and, as P&E Chair, presided over and advanced the 2026 marriage-equality amendment (HJ3) -> biblical_marriage q0 FALSE (her individual floor roll call was behind the new LIS app, but her authorship/leadership of the progressive slate is documented; q1 left null to avoid double-counting the less-isolated cell). Family/child, Christian-liberty, economic, immigration, federal-overreach: no documented Price-specific position -> null.

0.note: ReproRising 2025 A+/'Champion' (scores actual votes); co-patron of the reproductive-freedom amendment HJ1 and chief patron of the Right to Contraception Act (HB1716) -- does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Pro-abortion-access record (ReproRising A+) -- opposite of abortion-abolition.

0.note: Openly pansexual; as Chair of House Privileges & Elections, presided over and advanced the 2026 marriage-equality amendment (HJ3) repealing the one-man-one-woman clause -- does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.
CHIEF PATRON of HB968 (2026), which bars hand-counting of machine-readable ballots except on scanner failure -- directly contrary to hand-counted paper ballots.

1.note: CHIEF PATRON of the Voting Rights Act of Virginia (HB1890, 2021) expanding ballot access -- opposite of photo-ID/citizenship-verification restrictions.

2.note: Chief patron of the felon-re-enfranchisement amendment; P&E Chair expanding voting access -- contrary to single-day in-person/limited-absentee posture.

3.note: Record of expanding ballot access (VRA of Virginia, felon re-enfranchisement) -- contrary to opposing mass mail-in/early-voting expansion.

1.note: Patron of HB1920 (sentence reconsideration/reduction after transfer to Corrections -- a 'second-look' leniency measure) -- contrary to opposing soft-on-crime policy.
Giffords-endorsed; chief sponsor of legislation authorizing localities to enact their own gun-violence-prevention/firearm measures -- contrary to opposing gun restrictions.

profile.religion: Christian (official VA House bio; cited Romans 8:28 in interview); denomination not publicly stated
profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-86 (York County/Hampton/Poquoson). Freshman — won Nov 4, 2025 defeating incumbent Republican A.C. Cordoza; seated Jan 14, 2026; term through Jan 2028. U.S. Navy submarine-program technician via the Newport News Apprentice School; executive coach / community advocate.

virgil-thornton-sr.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-86 (D), FRESHMAN seated Jan 2026. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Thin record by design: scored from his own CITED public statements (interviews/campaign — labeled SOFT in research) plus one HARD chief-patron bill (HB773, 2026, expanding the absentee/provisional ballot-curing window; passed House 62-35). Stated public positions: backs the reproductive-freedom amendment and frames abortion as a decision for 'women, families, and their doctors' (endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates); names the marriage-equality amendment among his top-three priorities; 'strongly opposes diverting public funds to private or religious schools' (anti-school-choice); supports 'mandatory training and licensing for firearm ownership' (Giffords-endorsed). The four opening-day party-line amendment votes (HJ1/HJ2/HJ3) were NOT individually machine-confirmed, so they are NOT scored as his roll calls -- scoring rests on his explicit statements + HB773. Public dispute form is the backstop; this record should upgrade to HARD roll calls once he accrues a fuller voting history. Christian-liberty, public-justice, immigration ('did not answer'), federal-overreach: NO EVIDENCE -> null.

0.note: Stated position: pregnancy/contraception decisions 'should be left to women, families, and their doctors'; backs the reproductive-freedom amendment; endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates -- does not affirm personhood from conception. (His own public statements; freshman with no recorded abortion roll call yet.)

1.note: Pro-abortion-access stated position -- opposite of abortion-abolition.

0.note: Named the marriage-equality amendment among his stated top-three legislative priorities -- does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: Pledged to help pass the same-sex-marriage amendment with the Democratic majority.

0.note: 'Strongly opposes diverting public funds to private or religious schools' -- contrary to universal school choice / homeschool-funding freedom.

2.note: CHIEF PATRON of HB773 (2026) extending the absentee/provisional ballot-curing deadline (passed House 62-35) -- expands absentee voting, contrary to single-day/limited-absentee posture.

3.note: HB773 widens the window to count mailed/absentee ballots -- contrary to opposing mass mail-in expansion.

0.note: Supports 'mandatory training and licensing requirements for firearm ownership' (compares it to a driver's license) -- contrary to permitless/constitutional carry.

profile.religion: Not publicly stated (no denomination found in Wikipedia, Ballotpedia, or campaign bio)
profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-87 (Hampton) since Jan 2004; represented HD-92 (2004-2024) before the 2023 redistricting. Chair of the House Labor & Commerce Committee. Former middle-school teacher; educated at Thomas Nelson Community College and Christopher Newport University. Reelected Nov 4, 2025.

jeion-ward.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-87 (D), long-tenure (since 2004); Chair of Labor & Commerce. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Reliable progressive with a FULL roll-call record (pulled from LIS). Voted YEA on all three readings of the abortion (HJ1/SJ247), marriage (HJ9/SJ249/HJ3/HB612/SB311), and felon-voting (HJ2) measures; ReproRising A+/'Champion'. CHIEF PATRON of the $15 minimum-wage bills in both 2025 (HB1928) and 2026 (HB1); lead labor patron. Comprehensive gun-control voting (AWB+magazine ban HB1607/HB217, public-carry ban HB1524, 5-day waiting period, red-flag expansion, storage mandates). Voted YEA on recreational-marijuana retail market (HB642) and on HB1441/SB783 (restricting local cooperation with federal immigration authorities). GENUINE CROSS-PATTERN CREDIT (documented, not a hit-list): voted YEA on HB131 (2026) requiring public higher-ed to provide reasonable accommodations for religious beliefs (94-2) -> christian_liberty q1 TRUE. No school-choice/ESA, CBDC, or 10th-Amendment record -> null. Several raw 'NAY' votes are procedural rejections of weakened Senate substitutes, consistent with her progressive position -- not miscoded as conservative.

0.note: Voted YEA on the reproductive-freedom amendment (HJ1 2025 & 2026; SJ247 2025); ReproRising A+/'Champion' -- does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Pro-abortion-access record (ReproRising A+) -- opposite of abortion-abolition.

0.note: Voted YEA on the same-sex-marriage amendment (HJ9/SJ249 2025; HJ3 2026) and on the statutory marriage-equality bills (HB612/SB311 2026) -- does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: Voted YEA to codify same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution and statute.

Voted YEA on HB131 (2026) requiring public higher-education institutions to provide reasonable accommodations for students' religious beliefs (94-2) -- supports religious-conscience accommodation. (Documented credit despite an otherwise progressive record.)

2.note: CHIEF PATRON of the $15 minimum-wage bills (HB1928 2025; HB1 2026) plus a broad pro-labor spending slate -- expansionary wage-mandate posture.

1.note: Voted YEA on the felon-re-enfranchisement amendment (HJ2) expanding the franchise -- opposite of photo-ID/citizenship-verification restrictions.

2.note: Voted YEA on absentee/early-voting expansion (HB2056 2025; HB773/HB774/SB438 2026 adding early-voting Sundays) -- contrary to single-day/limited-absentee posture.

3.note: Voted YEA on absentee/early-voting expansion -- contrary to opposing mass mail-in/early voting.

1.note: Voted YEA on HB642/SB542 (2026) establishing a retail recreational-marijuana market -- decriminalization direction, contrary to opposing soft-on-crime policy.

2.note: Voted YEA on HB1441/SB783 (2026) restricting/conditioning local law-enforcement agreements with federal immigration authorities -- a sanctuary-leaning posture, contrary to opposing sanctuary policy.

0.note: Voted YEA on a 5-day firearm waiting period (HB2631 2025) -- contrary to permitless/constitutional carry.

1.note: Voted YEA on the assault-firearms+magazine ban (HB1607 2025, HB217 2026), public-carry ban (HB1524), red-flag/'substantial risk' expansion (HB896/HB901/HB1096), and mandatory secure-storage penalties -- comprehensive gun-control record.
